reforms.description: The Australian government is establishing a national voluntary certification scheme and set of standards for modern methods of construction (MMC) to provide regulatory certainty and facilitate economies of scale in the construction sector.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By streamlining regulations and promoting MMC, the initiative aims to enhance productivity and reduce costs in the construction sector, ultimately supporting economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: A national voluntary certification initiative for manufacturers of MMC is underway.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiative represents a significant step towards modernizing construction practices in Australia, which could lead to improved efficiency and productivity in the sector.
reforms.source_quote: A national voluntary certification initiative for manufacturers of MMC, which proposes to provide uniform national definitions of MMC for inclusion in the National Construction Code and streamline compliance, is also underway.

reforms.description: The Australian government is promoting interoperability and digital adoption by mandating Building Information Modelling (BIM) for government-funded projects and supporting its uptake among smaller firms.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Mandating BIM is expected to enhance efficiency and collaboration in construction projects, leading to better resource allocation and productivity gains.
reforms.status_evidence: The government can promote interoperability and digital adoption by mandating BIM for government-funded projects.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative is a significant move towards integrating digital tools in construction, which can lead to improved project outcomes and efficiency.
reforms.source_quote: The government can promote interoperability and digital adoption by mandating BIM for government-funded projects and supporting its uptake among smaller firms.

reforms.description: Australia is developing a voluntary national certification scheme for modern methods of construction (MMC) manufacturers to enhance productivity and sustainability in the housing sector.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The certification scheme aims to standardize practices and improve efficiency in construction, potentially leading to lower costs and increased housing supply.
reforms.status_evidence: The scheme is currently under development by the Australian Building Codes Board.
reforms.importance_rationale: The certification scheme represents a significant step towards modernizing construction practices in Australia, with potential long-term benefits for productivity and sustainability.
reforms.source_quote: Australia could foster uptake by creating a voluntary national certification scheme for MMC manufacturers (currently under development by the Australian Building Codes Board).

reforms.description: The federal government has created the Housing Australia Future Fund, an investment vehicle aimed at increasing social and affordable housing, with an initial target of supporting the construction of 40,000 homes over five years.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increases the supply of affordable housing, which can enhance social equity and support economic stability.
reforms.status_evidence: The fund is operational and aims to support the construction of social and affordable homes.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant commitment to addressing the affordable housing crisis, with potential long-term impacts on social equity and economic stability.
reforms.source_quote: The federal government has also created the Housing Australia Future Fund, a dedicated investment vehicle to increase social and affordable housing.

reforms.description: The Australian Government expanded the 5% Deposit Scheme to allow all first homebuyers to purchase homes with a minimal deposit, aiming to increase homeownership accessibility.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Facilitates homeownership for first-time buyers, potentially stimulating the housing market and related economic activities.
reforms.status_evidence: The scheme was expanded to give all first homebuyers the ability to buy with a 5% deposit.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform significantly enhances access to homeownership for first-time buyers, addressing a critical barrier in the housing market.
reforms.source_quote: The Australian Government 5% Deposit Scheme was expanded in October 2025 to give all first homebuyers the ability to buy with a 5% deposit.

reforms.description: The 2024-25 Budget provided an estimated AUD 22.7 billion over ten years to accelerate investment in priority industries, including renewable hydrogen and clean energy technologies.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This funding is expected to enhance investment in clean energy technologies, which can lead to increased productivity and innovation in the energy sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The budget allocated significant funding to support clean energy initiatives.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a substantial commitment to clean energy investment, which is critical for Australia's transition to a low-carbon economy and has long-term implications for economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: The 2024-25 Budget provided an estimated AUD 22.7 billion over ten years to accelerate investment in priority industries, including renewable hydrogen, green metals, low-carbon liquid fuels, refining and processing of critical minerals and manufacturing of clean energy technologies.

reforms.description: The government allocated AUD 63.8 million over ten years to support agricultural emission reduction efforts, including upskilling farmers and land managers.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Investing in agricultural research and development can lead to more sustainable practices and improved productivity in the sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The budget contained allocations for agricultural emission reduction efforts.
reforms.importance_rationale: This funding is significant for enhancing agricultural practices but is part of a broader strategy rather than a standalone major reform.
reforms.source_quote: The 2024-25 Budget contained AUD 63.8 million over ten years to support initial agricultural emission reduction efforts.

reforms.description: The federal government published its first National Battery Strategy, identifying manufacturing energy storage systems as a high-value opportunity for Australia’s domestic battery manufacturing industry, backed by substantial funding.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The strategy aims to enhance domestic manufacturing capabilities in energy storage, which can drive innovation and economic growth in the renewable energy sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The National Battery Strategy was published and is backed by funding.
reforms.importance_rationale: The National Battery Strategy represents a significant shift towards enhancing Australia's capabilities in energy storage, which is crucial for the transition to renewable energy and has broad economic implications.
reforms.source_quote: In May 2024 the federal government published its first National Battery Strategy (NBS), which identifies manufacturing energy storage systems as a high-value opportunity for Australia’s domestic battery manufacturing industry.

reforms.description: The government is providing AUD 40 million to deliver nationwide public kerbside and fast EV charge points as part of the Net Zero Plan.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Investing in EV charging infrastructure supports the adoption of electric vehicles, which can lead to reduced emissions and stimulate economic activity in the green technology sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The Government is providing AUD 40 million to deliver nationwide public kerbside and fast EV charge points.
reforms.importance_rationale: This investment in EV charging infrastructure is a significant step towards supporting the transition to electric vehicles, which is essential for meeting emissions targets.
reforms.source_quote: As part of the Net Zero Plan announced in September 2025, the Government is providing AUD 40 million to deliver nationwide public kerbside and fast EV charge points.

reforms.description: The AUD 1 billion Green Iron Investment Fund will support the transformation of the Whyalla Steelworks and early mover green iron projects across Australia.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This fund aims to enhance the competitiveness of Australia's green iron sector, fostering innovation and investment in sustainable production methods.
reforms.status_evidence: The AUD 1 billion Green Iron Investment Fund, announced in February 2025, will support two streams of funding.
reforms.importance_rationale: This fund represents a significant investment in a key sector for Australia's economic future, aiming to position the country as a leader in green iron production.
reforms.source_quote: The AUD 1 billion Green Iron Investment Fund, announced in February 2025, will support two streams of funding: AUD 500 million will be available to the support the transformation of the Whyalla Steelworks, and the National Development Stream includes at least AUD 500 million for early mover green iron projects across Australia.

reforms.description: The AUD 1.5 billion Future Made in Australia Innovation Fund is set to start this year, aimed at fostering innovation in priority sectors.
reforms.component_name: Future Made in Australia Innovation Fund
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This fund is designed to stimulate innovation across various sectors, enhancing productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The AUD 1.5 billion Future Made in Australia Innovation Fund is only due to start this year.
reforms.importance_rationale: This fund represents a major commitment to innovation funding, targeting key sectors that are crucial for Australia's economic transformation.
reforms.source_quote: The AUD 1.5 billion Future Made in Australia Innovation Fund is only due to start this year.

reforms.description: The revitalised National Competition Policy (NCP) aims to reverse slowing productivity and wages growth by targeting regulatory barriers across sectors and facilitating a single national market.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By reducing regulatory barriers, the NCP is expected to enhance competition, leading to increased productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The first tranche of reforms was agreed in 2024.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a comprehensive overhaul of competition policy, aiming to significantly enhance productivity and economic integration across Australia.
reforms.source_quote: In 2024, Australian jurisdictions agreed to Australia’s most comprehensive competition policy reform agenda in over two decades.

reforms.description: The Automatic Mutual Recognition (AMR) reform allows licensees to practice in other jurisdictions without re-applying, aiming to enhance labor mobility and reduce barriers in occupational licensing.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By simplifying licensing processes, AMR is expected to improve labor mobility, thereby enhancing productivity and economic efficiency.
reforms.status_evidence: AMR commenced in July 2021 in several jurisdictions.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform significantly streamlines occupational licensing, which is crucial for enhancing labor mobility and addressing skill shortages.
reforms.source_quote: A 2021 reform introduced Automatic Mutual Recognition (AMR), giving a licensee a legal right to practise in another participating jurisdiction.

reforms.description: Australia is set to implement reforms to lower barriers to the adoption of international and overseas standards, which will facilitate competition and reduce compliance costs for firms.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By reducing compliance costs and facilitating market entry for foreign suppliers, these reforms are expected to enhance competition and variety in the market.
reforms.status_evidence: Reforms to lower barriers to the adoption of international and overseas standards are among the reform priorities eligible for the first tranche of NCP payments to be distributed in 2025.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it addresses compliance burdens that hinder competition, but it is part of a broader set of reforms under the National Competition Policy.
reforms.source_quote: Among the reform priorities eligible for the first tranche of NCP payments to be distributed in 2025 is work by the states and territories on reforms to lower barriers to the adoption of international and overseas standards.

reforms.description: Reforms to the Sydney Airport Demand Management framework were implemented to enhance slot compliance and promote competition in the domestic aviation sector.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By improving slot allocation and compliance, these reforms are expected to increase competition among airlines, leading to lower fares and better service for consumers.
reforms.status_evidence: Reforms to the Sydney Airport Demand Management framework were implemented in 2025.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it addresses competition issues in a concentrated market, but it is part of a broader aviation reform package.
reforms.source_quote: Reforms to the Sydney Airport Demand Management (SADM) framework were implemented in 2025.
